ruling.decision: Option 1 — expand rag-index analyzer file coverage
ruling.rationale_summary: Honest single source of truth (validation-report.header_missing_count canonical); the 47 residual files belong in retrieval (type-only + tests); AC-09 stays meaningful end-to-end; cons bounded; DR-PHASE-7-B holds with zero schema delta.

problem_statement: After path-normalization fix (WO-INDEXING-ORCHESTRATOR-PATH-NORMALIZATION-001, archive 4827b61), AC-09 dynamic alignment is 215/262 chunks correctly skipped (17.9% gap, outside locked ±10% band). Root cause is structural: rag-index's analyzerService.analyze(...) covers ~215 of the 262 missing-header files. The 47-file shortfall is primarily type-only modules and __tests__/ files — present in .coderef/graph.json (populate-coderef element-grain scan) but absent from rag-index's analyzer graph.

T1_analyzer_glob_patterns.hypothesis: The glob list is sufficient to match all 47 files on disk (test-files end in .ts/.tsx, type-only files end in .ts), so glob coverage is likely NOT the gap. Verify by globbing the patterns directly and confirming all 47 names appear.
T1_analyzer_glob_patterns.investigation_method: Run a node script that globs the same patterns from the same cwd and asserts membership of all 47 residual filenames.

T2_analyzer_internal_filter.hypothesis: Even with matching globs, the analyzer's element extractor may skip files that produce no extractable elements (type-only files, certain test patterns). This would mean files reach the analyzer but produce zero nodes, getting dropped before ChunkConverter sees them.
T2_analyzer_internal_filter.investigation_method: Probe analyzerService.analyze output directly; check whether the 47 residual filenames appear in the returned node list at all (with any node count, including zero).

T3_chunk_converter_filter_chain.hypothesis: ChunkConverter has a filter chain (fileExists check + chunk-eligibility filter). If files reach ChunkConverter but produce no eligible chunks (e.g., element type not in the chunk-eligible enum), they vanish at the conversion stage rather than the analyzer stage.
T3_chunk_converter_filter_chain.investigation_method: Probe ChunkConverter output for the 47 residual filenames; instrument or trace which filter (if any) drops them.

expected_root_cause_distribution: T2 (analyzer internal filter) is highest-probability for type-only files (no functions/classes for the element extractor to anchor on). T3 (chunk-converter filter) is highest-probability for __tests__/ files (chunk-eligibility may exclude test patterns). T1 (glob) is lowest-probability — investigated for completeness, not expected to be the cause.
fix_strategy_constraints: Investigate ALL THREE targets first; do NOT preemptively change all three. Identify the 1-2 actual gap-source(s) and adjust them.
Prefer the smallest blast radius that closes the gap — if T2 alone explains 35/47, fix T2 first and reassess T3.
If T3 fix would require widening chunk-eligibility to include element types currently excluded by design, FILE A NEW STUB — do NOT widen unilaterally.

If T2 fix would require changing element-extraction behavior in shared analyzer code (not rag-index local code), HALT and report — analyzer-side changes affect populate-coderef and would risk reopening Phase 1.
Zero changes to IndexingResult schema (DR-PHASE-7-B locked).

decision_records.title: Investigation order: T1 → T2 → T3
decision_records.decision: Run T1 (glob check) first as a 5-minute verification step; expected NO-OP. Then T2 (analyzer internal output) and T3 (ChunkConverter output) in parallel during Phase 1.
decision_records.rationale: T1 is cheap and rules out a whole hypothesis class. T2 and T3 are the actual likely sources; running them in parallel saves a phase boundary.

decision_records.title: Boundary: rag-index local code only
decision_records.decision: All fixes ship inside src/integration/rag/* (indexing-orchestrator.ts, chunk-converter.ts, ChunkConverter helpers). NO changes to shared analyzer code (src/analyzer/*) or scanner code (src/scanner/*).
decision_records.rationale: Shared analyzer/scanner changes affect populate-coderef and risk reopening Phase 1 / Phase 5 archives. The rebuild is COMPLETE; this is post-rebuild maintenance.

decision_records.title: AC-02 target: ≥235/262 (matches predecessor ±10% band)
decision_records.decision: Success criterion is ≥235 chunks correctly skipped (matches the ±10% lower-bound threshold the predecessor WO held to). Hitting 262/262 is preferred but not required if last-mile coverage requires shared-analyzer changes.
decision_records.rationale: ±10% is the original AC-09 contract from Phase 7; preserving that threshold is the goal. Going beyond 235 is bonus.

decision_records.title: If 235 unreachable inside rag-index local code, file STUB and close DIVERGED
decision_records.decision: If investigation finds the 47-file gap can ONLY be closed by changes to shared analyzer/scanner code, the WO closes with AC-02 DIVERGED (mirroring predecessor pattern) and a new STUB-ANALYZER-SHARED-EXTRACTOR-COVERAGE-001 is filed for an analyzer-side WO that ORCHESTRATOR scopes separately.
decision_records.rationale: DR-ANALYZER-SLICE-B is load-bearing; we don't break it to chase a metric.

decision_records.title: Test fixtures: type-only file + __tests__ file
decision_records.decision: Add at least one type-only fixture (no functions/classes) and one __tests__/ fixture to the existing chunk-conversion test suite. Assert each produces ≥1 chunk after the fix.
decision_records.rationale: These are the two structural gap categories. Direct fixtures prevent regression even if the analyzer/converter is later refactored.

risks.description: T2 fix requires shared-analyzer change — would reopen Phase 1 risk.
risks.mitigation: DR-ANALYZER-SLICE-B + halt-and-report rule. Close WO DIVERGED + file new stub.

risks.description: T3 fix widens chunk-eligibility and creates retrieval noise from test files.
risks.mitigation: Existing --layer/--capability filters from Phase 7 mitigate noise per query. If retrieval-noise complaints surface in production use, scope a follow-up WO; do not pre-optimize.

risks.description: Expanding coverage exposes latent ChunkConverter bugs on previously-untested chunk shapes.
risks.mitigation: The new test fixtures (DR-ANALYZER-SLICE-E) catch shape bugs early. Existing invariant tests from predecessor WO catch join bugs. tsc --noEmit clean is the type-shape gate.

risks.description: Embedding cost / index size growth from +47 files makes local Ollama runs slow.
risks.mitigation: One-time cost on local Ollama; rag-index supports --reset and reuse. If runtime regression is significant, document in ARCHIVED.md; do not block merge.

risks.description: Investigation reveals a 6th category of files (beyond type-only + tests) not covered by either fixture.
risks.mitigation: Expected — diff-skipped.mjs + probe-analyzer.mjs (preserved in predecessor archive) enumerate the full 47. Add fixture(s) for the additional category before close.

key_files_to_touch: src/integration/rag/indexing-orchestrator.ts (analyzer invocation site if T1/T2 fix required)
src/integration/rag/chunk-converter.ts (filter chain if T3 fix required)
key_files_to_read_only: src/analyzer/analyzer-service.ts (or wherever analyzerService.analyze lives — read to understand internal filter, NOT to modify)
halt_and_report_points: Phase 1 investigation reveals ALL THREE targets are insufficient (gap is in shared analyzer/scanner code) → close DIVERGED per DR-ANALYZER-SLICE-D
Phase 2 fix would require widening chunk-eligibility enum to include currently-excluded element types → file new stub, halt
Phase 2 fix would require changes outside src/integration/rag/* → halt per DR-ANALYZER-SLICE-B
Phase 3 dynamic re-verify shows chunksSkipped < 235 with all in-scope fixes applied → close DIVERGED per DR-ANALYZER-SLICE-C/D
Any phase: introducing IndexingResult schema field → halt (DR-PHASE-7-B violation)
Any phase: introducing new SkipReason enum value → halt (out-of-scope)

target_post_wo_state.ac_09_dynamic: ≥235/262 (preferred: 262/262 if reachable inside rag-index local code)

post_close_followups: If WO closes with AC-02 DIVERGED, file STUB-ANALYZER-SHARED-EXTRACTOR-COVERAGE-001 capturing exactly which residual files require shared-analyzer changes.
Update Phase 7 ARCHIVED.md cross-reference to point to this WO's archive (ASSISTANT-side patch, not CORE — Phase 7 archive itself stays untouched).
Optional: if 262/262 lands cleanly, capture the win in MEMORY project file as a coda to the rebuild-complete entry.
